comments.text: Hey, CBP isn't allowed to do any of the stuff it's doing in Minneapolis even within the confines of its extremely ridiculous "100 miles from any border" jurisdiction. It's 300 miles from Canada and 160 miles to Lake Superior. They're breaking the law standing around, let alone beating up protesters.

A few days ago, I looked at how the international border through The Great Lakes was determined.
But what about the border west of Lake Superior?
It took decades to determine and wasn't just "drawn with a ruler by some guy".
Let's learn more! 🧵1/13
Because CBP defines each of the great lakes as an international waterway, thus its 100 mile enforcement zone begins at the lakeshore and extends 100 miles.
i gotta ask... if lake Michigan (surrounded by United states) is a border shouldn't the whole Mississippi River system also be a border

For Reference, the black line is the international border with Canada. Lake Michigan is 100% within the United states, The closest border of the US to Illinois is about 200 miles away. The Border Patrol has no business being anywhere in Illinois.

Notable that Minneapolis is the rare metro more than 100 miles from the border or the coast (Great Lakes count), an operating boundary that lets CBP operate normally far beyond what anyone reasonable considers the border.

A reform that might be possible in under 3 years is to (re)define CPB’s role. They are currently supposed to only act within 100 miles of a border and no matter how loosely you define that, Minneapolis is still more than 150 from Great Lakes.
the fact that Chicago is considered within the border zone is ridiculous. Lake Michigan is famously the only great lake that's entirely within the US

People should know that the reason CBP
Is allowed to operate in Chicago is that the federal government has determined that the Chicgao shores of Lake Michigan, over 450 miles from Canada, as a “border.” 🙄

Not many sneak into USA via the Chicago River or Lake Michigan (the only Great Lake that doesn't border any other country). But armed feds tried to intimidate our city... by joining the architecture tour crowds. Real tough guys. (Photos: Block Club Chicago & Chicago Sun-Times)
ACLU info. Particularly important in border states and coastal states. Michigan peeps, this covers most of the state due to the Great Lakes.

CBP considers Chicago to be in the 100 mile zone b/c they consider all the Great Lakes' shorelines, not the actual borders, to be international borders including, absurdly, Lake Michigan. Any truly textualist or originalist jurist would see this as preposterous. www.aclu.org/documents/co...

tRump is using the Great Lakes shoreline as the starting point for the 100 mile rule. Unjustified! Also, 100 mile radius around every international airport 🛫 within the country is considered fair game for I.C.E. and border control. Unjustified!
If there's not a LAW set in stone, tRump exploits it.
Fascinating title for the article… as though Lake Superior isn’t bisected by an international border. 😏
www.chicagotribune.com/2025/09/25/i... Now we're paying boarder control to sight see. Lake Michigan has no international boarders.
They do patrol international lakes like this, Great Lakes, etc but crossing the border is a gross violation of sovereignty.
The Border Patrol is not even supposed to be there. Lake Michigan is not the border.
how the fuck is chicago within the 200 mile border zone if lake michigan isn't considered an international lake

The Border Patrol is legally allowed to operate only within 100 miles of a U.S. border, including coastlines. Last time I checked, Minneapolis was about 150 miles from Duluth on Lake Superior, and even farther from the Canadian border.
The effing moron doesn’t even know that no part of Lake Michigan borders on Canada. www.ctvnews.ca/politics/art...

The blue zone is what CBP wants everyone to believe is the 100 mile border zone. The red lines delineate the actual zone based on the US External Boundary. Big differences for Great Lakes areas and DC.
This reminds me: LAKE MICHIGAN IS ENTIRELY SOVEREIGN US TERRITORY. CHICAGO IS NOT PART OF THIS ALREADY DUMB FUCKING DEFINITION OF THE BOARDER.

Surrounded by the Great Lakes, almost every place in Michigan is within 100 miles of a border - according to DHS guidelines. Despite that idiotic ruling, the majority of Michiganders treasure our special relationship with Canada, much like other states that share that peaceful border. #AbolishICE
I want somebody to explain to me why Lake Michigan, an entirely American pond, counts for determining the 100 mile zone

Of course the big question is why is CBP even in ChicagoLand? Any 3rd grader knows Lake Michigan does not touch any other country's boarder. Not ever close. And Illinois does not touch any other country's boarder But does his demented dictator knows that? 2028 oh please!!

ICE guys should be able to protect themselves. And contrary to what CBP thinks, Lake Michigan does not border Canada. It’s the only one of the five Great Lakes that is fully inside the US. So take your bat and ball, and go away.

He does need to workshop this talking point, though. He made a sarcastic remark about Lake Michigan and Canada that made them sound as if they share a border. They do not. No part of Canada is closer to Chicago than 250 miles away. Chicago is not in the 100-mile external border zone.
Finally someone else is not buying the border crap from CBP. Lake Michigan is in the US totally. Illinois does not border Canada. CBP has no legit claim to be in Chicago

I just travelled 2,000 miles from the tristate border up to the Great Lakes. They were not checking the buses nor stations. Just typing this for no reason.

the idea that our borders are this universally impenetrable thing is a belief almost entirely unmoored from the reality of most people living in border states. the great lakes are just...there. people land on miami beaches every day. i know people who flew back less than a week after being deported.
Not that it's important in the big picture, but Lake Michigan is the only one of the Great Lakes that does NOT border Canada.

in the first place? Lake Michigan is located
entirely within the USA, right? Send them all to Texas &

US Border Patrol has NO jurisdiction in Charlotte NC - it's not within 100 miles of any border. And we need to change the Great Lakes bullshit that defines Chicago within 100 miles of a border. In fact, the entire "100 mile Border Zone" is not even a law - it's an arbitrary number from 1953.

The basic borders were North Ave, Lake Michigan, 22nd St (Cermak Rd), and Wood St. Obviously the shoreline was much further west than it is today. The South Branch also took a slightly different course before being straightened in the late 1920s.
Regarding the potential Hammond, Indiana, Bears, the spiritual distance from Chicago is much farther than the physical distance. The two cities border each other along the Lake Michigan shoreline.

Duckworth explains why these thugs are allowed to roam so widely: they're allowed to be active within 100 air miles of a border; Lake Michigan is considered a "border." (wut) Her suggestion to limit their activities to 1 air mile of the "border" is good, though won't help much of Chicagoland.

JB Pritzker just said that CBP is only authorized to act within 100
Miles of a border. CBP is saying Lake Michigan is a border as an excuse to invade Chicago
They’re asserting they’re within the 100 mile zone because of Lake Michigan. That’s obviously a profoundly dumb & dishonest claim, so why did they say it? So they could do stop & seizures in IL?

Possibly because of the Customs and Border Patrol's jurisdiction to search and detain up to 100 miles inland from any U.S. border. Chicago is a disputed area because it is more than 100 miles from Canada, but the Great Lakes are recognized as international boundary waters. Regardless, CBP sucks.

the russ-bagot treaty prohibits both the united states and canada (which inherited treaty obligations from the empire) from putting warships on the four shared great lakes or the st lawrence seaway. but lake michigan isn’t shared, and wisconsin has no international borders.

Lake Michigan, despite being entirely within the US, is treated by ICE as international waters (even tho it absolutely isn't; only the other 4 are), and so they claim the inner shores of each of the Great Lakes, including Lake Michigan, as the starting point for the 100-mile zone measurement inland.

The border trade is boosted by the Chicago Riviera, the Michigan shore of Lake Michigan, where many Chicagoans spend their Summer vacations. Traversing the Northwest Indiana Free Trade Zone is part of the drive.
They’ve declared the coast of Great Lakes the border, MPLS is 150 miles from Superior. It’s 50 miles more than their 100 mile border range, but do they really care about laws?

There's an international border on Lake Champlain, just like in the Great Lakes, so the USCG patrols it. I imagine if there are state or national parks in the area they'd also have some kind of presence.

Noting that right now, their definition of the border includes the Great Lakes and Atlantic and Pacific Oceans. That needs to be removed from the area where they are allowed to operate.
I know we're way past "the rules" but even by the extremely bullshit stance that the shore of the great lakes count as a border, MSP is farther than 100miles.

In creating the boundary, a map from John Mitchell in 1755 was used which depicted Lake of the Woods as egg-shaped. Mitchell also theorized that a line west of that point intersected the Mississippi. This belief was the first step towards creating the border blip. 🧵4/12

🌍 Did you know that... The two countries that share the largest border are the United States and Canada. The international boundary between these two nations stretches approximately 8,891 km (5,525 mi), including both the land border and the maritime borders in the Great Lakes region. #Geography
